 <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B></B>BIOVEST INTERNATIONAL, INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES<B> </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B></B>NOTES TO CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS<B> </B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B></B>FOR THE YEARS ENDED SEPTEMBER 30, 2011 AND 2010<B> </B></FONT></P> <p STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></P>
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>11. Long-term debt (continued) </B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>Class 8, Unsecured Option A Obligations: </I></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px;padding-bottom:0px;"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">On the Effective Date, the
Company became obligated to the Company&#146;s unsecured creditors in the original aggregate principal amount of approximately $2.7 million in cash together with interest at five percent (5%)&nbsp;per annum to be paid in one installment on
March&nbsp;27, 2014 (the &#147;Option A Obligations&#148;). These unsecured creditors&#146; claims include, but are not limited to the Pulaski Bank notes, the Southwest Bank note, and the related guarantor indemnities. As of September&nbsp;30, 2011,
this obligation increased by $0.06&nbsp;million due to an amendment made to the Company&#146;s listing of unsecured creditors, allowing a previously unfiled claim for professional services rendered with respect to the clinical trial for BiovaxID<FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUP STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; bottom:.8ex">&reg;</SUP></FONT>. The fair value of the Option A Obligations was recorded against the carrying value of each claim holder electing an
Option A distribution as of the Effective Date, resulting in a $0.4 million gain on reorganization for the year ended September&nbsp;30, 2011. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>Class 8, Unsecured Option C Notes: </I></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">On
the Effective Date, the Company became obligated to certain of its unsecured creditors in the original aggregate principal amount of approximately $2.0 million. Each such unsecured creditor received an amount equal to 100% of such unsecured
creditors&#146; allowed Class 8 unsecured claim (including post-petition interest under the Plan at the rate of three percent (3%)&nbsp;per annum) in a combination of debt and equity resulting in the issuance of a total of $1.8 million in new notes
(the &#147;Option C Notes&#148;), as well as 0.2&nbsp;million shares of the Company&#146;s common stock, using an effective conversion rate equal to $1.66 per share. The Option C Notes bear interest at seven percent (7%)&nbsp;and are convertible
into the Company&#146;s common stock in seven quarterly installments beginning on February&nbsp;17, 2011 as follows: </FONT></P> <P STYLE="font-size:6px;margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px">&nbsp;</P>

<FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">)&nbsp;of the Option C Notes plus accrued interest will be automatically converted into shares of the Company&#146;s common stock at a conversion rate equal to the Ten Day VWAP;

<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top"> <P ALIGN="left"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">should the Ten Day VWAP be less than $1.00 per share, the Option C Notes will not automatically convert into shares of the Company&#146;s common stock,

the Ten Day VWAP used for the initial conversion on the Effective Date ($1.66 per share). </FONT></P></TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">On February&nbsp;17, 2011,
May&nbsp;17, 2011, and again on August&nbsp;17, 2011 with the Ten Day VWAP at less than $1.00 per share, the holders of the Option C Notes elected to convert one-eighth of the Option C Notes plus accrued interest into shares of the Company&#146;s
common stock at a conversion rate equal to $1.00 per share, resulting in the aggregate issuance of 901,645 shares of the Company&#146;s common stock. </FONT></P>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B></B>BIOVEST INTERNATIONAL, INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES<B> </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B></B>NOTES TO CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS<B> </B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B></B>FOR THE YEARS ENDED SEPTEMBER 30, 2011 AND 2010<B> </B></FONT></P> <p STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></P>
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>11. Long-term debt (continued) </B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>Exit Financing: </I></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">On October&nbsp;19, 2010, the Company completed a financing as part of its
Plan (the &#147;Exit Financing&#148;). Pursuant to the Exit Financing, the Company issued secured convertible notes in the original aggregate principal amount of $7.0 million (the &#147;Initial Notes&#148;) and warrants to purchase shares of the
Company&#146;s common stock warrants to a total of twelve (12)&nbsp;accredited investors (the &#147;Buyers&#148;). Pursuant to the Exit Financing, the Company issued two separate types of warrants to the Buyers, Series A Warrants (the &#147;Initial
Series A Warrants&#148;) and Series B Warrants (the&nbsp;&#147;Initial Series B Warrants&#148;). </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">On the Effective Date: (a)&nbsp;the Initial
Notes were exchanged pursuant to the terms of the Plan for new unsecured convertible notes (the&nbsp;&#147;Exchange Notes&#148;) in the original aggregate principal amount of $7.04 million, (b)&nbsp;the Initial Series A Warrants were exchanged
pursuant to the terms of the Plan for new warrants to purchase a like number of shares of Company common stock (the&nbsp;&#147;Series A Exchange Warrants&#148;), and (c)&nbsp;the Initial Series B Warrants were exchanged pursuant to the terms of the
Plan for new warrants to purchase a like number of shares of the Company&#146;s common stock (the &#147;Series B Exchange Warrants&#148;). </FONT></P>

May&nbsp;1, 2021 (the &#147;Notes&#148;). The Notes bear interest as follows (yielding an effective interest rate of 4.1%): </FONT></P> <P STYLE="font-size:6px;margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px">&nbsp;</P>

<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top"> <P ALIGN="left"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">the original principal amount of the Laurus/Valens Term B Notes was $4.16 million; </FONT></P></TD></TR></TABLE>
<P STYLE="font-size:6px;margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">

<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top"> <P ALIGN="left"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">the Laurus/Valens Term B Notes mature on November&nbsp;17, 2013; </FONT></P></TD></TR></TABLE>
<P STYLE="font-size:6px;margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px">&nbsp;</P>

<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top"> <P ALIGN="left"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">interest accrues at the rate of eight (8%)&nbsp;percent per annum (with a twelve (12%)&nbsp;percent per annum default rate), and is payable at the time
of any principal payment or prepayment of principal; </FONT></P></TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="font-size:6px;margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px">&nbsp;</P>

<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top"> <P ALIGN="left"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">the Company may prepay the Laurus/Valens Term B Notes, without penalty, at any time; and </FONT></P></TD></TR></TABLE>
<P STYLE="font-size:6px;margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">

<TD WIDTH="1%" VALIGN="top"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top"> <P ALIGN="left"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">provided that the Laurus/Valens Term A Notes have been paid in full, the Company is required to make mandatory prepayments under the Laurus/Valens Term
B Notes from any intercompany funding by Accentia to the Company (with&nbsp;certain exceptions and conditions), but only up to the outstanding principal and accrued interest under the Laurus/Valens Term B Notes. </FONT></P></TD></TR></TABLE>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">With the prior written consent of Laurus/Valens, the Company may convert all or any portion of the outstanding principal and accrued interest under
either the Laurus/Valens Term A Notes or the Laurus/Valens Term B Notes into shares of the Company&#146;s common stock. The number of shares of the Company&#146;s common stock issuable on such a conversion is equal to (a)&nbsp;an amount equal to the
aggregate portion of the principal and accrued and unpaid interest thereon outstanding under the applicable Laurus/Valens Term A Notes or the Laurus/Valens Term B Notes being converted, divided by (b)&nbsp;ninety percent (90%)&nbsp;of the average
closing price publicly reported (or reported by Pink Sheets, LLC) for shares of the Company&#146;s common stock for the ten (10)&nbsp;trading days immediately preceding the date of the notice of conversion. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The Laurus/Valens Term A Notes and the Laurus/Valens Term B Notes are secured by a lien on all of the Company&#146;s assets, junior only to the lien
granted to Corps Real and to certain permitted liens. The Laurus/Valens Term A Notes and the Laurus/Valens Term B Notes are also guaranteed by Accentia (the &#147;Accentia Guaranty&#148;), up to a maximum amount of $4,991,360. The Accentia Guaranty
is secured by a pledge by Accentia of 20,115,818 shares of the Company&#146;s common stock owned by Accentia and by the assets of Accentia&#146;s subsidiary, Analytica International, Inc. (&#147;Analytica&#148;). On December 15, 2011, Accentia
closed on the definitive agreement selling the assets and business of Analytica. In connection with the Analytica Asset Purchase Agreement, Accentia entered into an agreement with LV Administrative Services, Inc. (&#147;LV&#148;), as agent for and
on behalf of Laurus/Valens, whereby Laurus/Valens, conditioned upon receipt of an upfront payment of the asset sales proceeds amended the terms of the Accentia Guaranty, whereby Laurus/Valens consented to the transactions contemplated by the
Analytica Asset Purchase Agreement and released all liens and security interests on Analytica&#146;s assets to be sold to the purchaser. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The
Laurus/Valens Term A and Term B Notes replaced the following obligations due to Laurus and its affiliates: </FONT></P> <P STYLE="font-size:6px;margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px">&nbsp;</P>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B></B>BIOVEST INTERNATIONAL, INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES<B> </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B></B>NOTES TO CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS<B> </B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B></B>FOR THE YEARS ENDED SEPTEMBER 30, 2011 AND 2010<B> </B></FONT></P> <p STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></P>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>12. Derivative liabilities </B></FONT></P>
 <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The Company does not use derivative financial instruments to hedge exposures to cash-flow, market or foreign-currency risks. However,
the Company and its consolidated subsidiaries have entered into certain other financial instruments and contracts, such as debt financing arrangements and freestanding warrants with features that are either (i)&nbsp;not afforded equity
classification, (ii)&nbsp;embody risks not clearly and closely related to host contracts, or (iii)&nbsp;may be net-cash settled by the counterparty. These instruments are required to be carried as derivative liabilities, at fair value. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The following table discloses the fair value of the Company&#146;s derivative liabilities and their location in the consolidated balance sheets as of
September&nbsp;30, 2011 and 2010. The Company held no asset derivatives at either reporting date. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="font-size:12px;margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px">&nbsp;</P>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>13. Liabilities subject to compromise </B></FONT></P>
 <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">On the Effective Date, the Company settled the majority of its pre-petition claims. Footnote 3 contains a summary of certain material
provisions of the Plan. On May&nbsp;10, 2011, the Company settled an outstanding Class 8 unsecured claim in the approximate amount of $0.772 million through the issuance of 493,001 shares of the Company&#146;s common stock valued at $0.55 per share.
The Company had established a reserve for this claim in the amount of $0.747 million. Settlement of the claim resulted in a gain of $0.476 million recorded as gain on reorganization on the Company&#146;s consolidated statement of operations for the
year ended September&nbsp;30, 2011. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">As of September&nbsp;30, 2011, some of the Company&#146;s Class 8 unsecured claims remain in dispute. A
reserve in the amount of $0.444&nbsp;million has been established representing the Company&#146;s estimate as to the eventual allowed amount of the aggregate claims. It is anticipated that these claims will be resolved through a combination of
issuance of the Company&#146;s common stock and long term debt through either a negotiated compromise or an adjudicated order entered in the Bankruptcy Court. </FONT></P>
 <P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>14. Stockholders&#146; equity </B></FONT></P>
 <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>Preferred stock: </I></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The
Company has authorized 50&nbsp;million shares of preferred stock. As of the fiscal years ended September&nbsp;30, 2011 and 2010, no preferred shares were issued or outstanding. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>Common stock: </I></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The Company has authorized 300&nbsp;million shares of common stock. For the
year ended September&nbsp;30, 2011, the Company issued the following: </FONT></P> <P STYLE="font-size:6px;margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px">&nbsp;</P>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>15. Common stock options and warrants </B></FONT></P>
 <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>Stock Option Plans: </I></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The Company&#146;s 2000 Stock Option Plan was approved and made effective by the Company&#146;s stockholders and Board of Directors on July&nbsp;19, 2000
(the &#147;2000 Plan&#148;). The Company&#146;s Amended and Restated 2006 Equity Incentive Plan (the &#147;2006 Plan&#148;) was approved by the Company&#146;s Board of Directors (the&nbsp;&#147;Board&#148;) on November&nbsp;2, 2006, and by the
written consent of holders of a majority of the Company&#146;s shares of common stock on October&nbsp;4, 2007. The Company&#146;s 2010 Equity Incentive Plan (the &#147;2010 Plan&#148;) was approved by the Board on November&nbsp;10, 2010, and by
holders of the majority of the Company&#146;s shares of common stock on January&nbsp;18, 2011. The 2000 Plan, 2006 Plan and 2010 Plan are collectively, the &#147;Stock Option Plans&#148;. The purposes of the Stock Option Plans are to create
incentives designed to motivate employees to significantly contribute toward the Company&#146;s growth and profitability, to provide the Company&#146;s executives, directors and other employees, and persons who, by their position, ability and
diligence, are able to make important contributions to the Company&#146;s growth and profitability, with an incentive to assist the Company in achieving its long-term corporate objectives, to attract and retain executives and other employees of
outstanding competence, and to provide such persons with an opportunity to acquire an equity interest in the Company. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The Stock Option Plans
are administered by a committee appointed by the Board or by the full Board of Directors. All members of such a committee must be non-employee directors and outside directors, as defined in the Stock Option Plans. Subject to the limitations set
forth in the Stock Option Plans, the administrator has the authority to grant options and to determine the purchase price of the shares of the Company&#146;s common stock covered by each option, the term of each option, the number of shares of the
Company&#146;s common stock to be covered by each option, to establish vesting schedules, to designate options as incentive stock options or non-qualified stock options, and to determine the persons to whom grants are to be made. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The common stock to be subject to awards under the Stock Option Plans may be either authorized and un-issued common stock shares or common stock shares
held in the treasury of the Company. The maximum number of shares of common stock which may be granted under the Stock Option Plans, subject to adjustment in accordance with the provisions of the Plans, is 37.0 million shares. For the purpose of
computing the total number of shares of stock remaining available for awards under the Stock Option Plans at any time while the Stock Option Plans are in effect, the total number of shares shall be reduced by: (1)&nbsp;the maximum number of common
stock shares subject to issuance upon exercise of outstanding options or outstanding stock appreciation rights granted under the Stock Option Plans and (2)&nbsp;the maximum number of shares related to outstanding other stock-based awards granted
under these Stock Option Plans, as determined by the Board in each case as of the dates on which such awards were granted. The common stock shares involved in the unexercised or undistributed portion of any terminated, expired or forfeited awards,
including any unexercised or undistributed portion of any terminated, expired or forfeited award under the 2000 or 2006 Plans will be made available for further Stock Option Plans awards. The Stock Option Plans permit the grant of restricted stock,
incentive stock options, non-qualified stock options, stock-appreciation rights, and phantom stock awards. Awards granted under the Stock Option Plans that have not vested will </FONT></P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">F-27
</FONT></P>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B></B>BIOVEST INTERNATIONAL, INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES<B> </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B></B>NOTES TO CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS<B> </B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B></B>FOR THE YEARS ENDED SEPTEMBER 30, 2011 AND 2010<B> </B></FONT></P> <p STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></P>
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>15. Common stock options and warrants (continued) </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>Stock Option Plans (continued): </I></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">generally terminate immediately upon the grantee&#146;s termination of employment or business relationship with us or any of the Company&#146;s
subsidiaries for any reason other than retirement, disability or death. The Board or a committee of the Board may determine at the time of the grant that an award agreement should contain provisions permitting the grantee to exercise the stock
options for any stated period after such termination, or for any period the Board or a committee of the Board determines to be advisable after the grantee&#146;s employment or business relationship with us terminates by reason of retirement,
disability, death or termination without cause. Except in the case of retirement or disability, vested Incentive Stock Options will generally terminate no more than three months after termination of the optionee&#146;s employment and twelve months
after termination of the optionee&#146;s employment due to disability. In the event of termination of employment as a result of retirement, termination of the Incentive Stock Option will occur at the end of the exercise period as specified in the
grant; and will comply in all other respects with the provisions of Code Section&nbsp;422. For purposes of the Stock Option Plans, &#147;retirement&#148; shall mean the employee terminates at a time when employee&#146;s combined age and years of
service equal&nbsp;at least sixty (60)&nbsp;(subject to the Age and Service Guidelines set forth below). </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The Age and Service Guidelines are:
</FONT></P> <P STYLE="font-size:6px;margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="4%"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></TD>
<TD WIDTH="4%" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">(a)</FONT></TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">the employee must have a minimum of three (3)&nbsp;years of service; and </FONT></TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="font-size:6px;margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="4%"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></TD>
<TD WIDTH="4%" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">(b)</FONT></TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">the employee must have attained a minimum age of fifty-five (55)&nbsp;years during the year of termination. </FONT></TD></TR></TABLE>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The Board or a committee of the Board may permit a deceased optionee&#146;s stock option grants to be exercised by the optionee&#146;s executor or heirs
during a period acceptable to the Board or a committee of the Board following the date of the optionee&#146;s death but such exercise must occur prior to the expiration date of the stock option grant. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Under GAAP with respect to the financial accounting treatment of stock options used to compensate employees, upon the grant of stock options under the
Stock Option Plans, the fair value of the options will be measured on the date of grant and this amount will be recognized as compensation expense ratably over the service period, which generally corresponds with the vesting period. Stock
appreciation rights granted under the Stock Option Plans must be settled in common stock. Therefore, stock appreciation rights granted under the Stock Option Plans will receive the same accounting treatment as options. The cash the Company receives
upon the exercise of stock options will be reflected as an increase in the Company&#146;s capital. No additional compensation expense will be recognized at the time stock options are exercised, although the issuance of shares of common stock upon
exercise may reduce basic earnings per share, as more shares of the Company&#146;s common stock would then be outstanding. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">In the case of a
grant of restricted stock, the fair value of the restricted stock award at the date of grant will be determined and this amount will be recognized over the service period of the award, usually the vesting period. The fair value of a restricted stock
award is equal to the fair market value of the Company&#146;s common stock on the date of grant. </FONT></P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">F-28
</FONT></P>

<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>


 <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B></B>BIOVEST INTERNATIONAL, INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES<B> </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B></B>NOTES TO CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS<B> </B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B></B>FOR THE YEARS ENDED SEPTEMBER 30, 2011 AND 2010<B> </B></FONT></P> <p STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></P>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>16. Segment information </B></FONT></P>
 <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px;padding-bottom:0px;"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The Company operates in three (3)&nbsp;identifiable industry segments. The Company&#146;s Cell Culture Products and
Services segment is engaged in the production and contract manufacturing of biologic drugs and cell production for research institutions worldwide. The Instruments and Disposables segment is engaged in the development, manufacture and marketing of
patented cell culture systems, equipment and consumable parts to pharmaceutical, diagnostic and biotechnology companies, as well as leading research institutions worldwide. The Therapeutic Vaccine segment is focused on developing BiovaxID<FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUP STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; bottom:.8ex">&reg;</SUP></FONT> and has received a federal grant in the amount of approximately $0.244 million under the Qualified Therapeutic
Discovery Project, as described earlier. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The Company defines its segment operating results as earnings/(loss) before general and
administrative costs, interest expense, interest income, other income, and income taxes. Under the Company&#146;s shared resources concept, assets of the Company are common to the segments listed below and thus not segregated for reporting purposes.
Goodwill was $2.1 million for the years ended September&nbsp;30, 2011 and 2010 and has not been allocated to the segments listed below. Segment information is as follows: </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="font-size:12px;margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px">&nbsp;</P>

<TD>&nbsp;</TD></TR>
</TABLE> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">ASC 740 requires that a deferred tax amount be reduced by a valuation allowance if, based on the weight of available
evidence it is more likely than not (a likelihood of more than fifty percent (50%))&nbsp;that some portion or all of the deferred tax assets will not be realized. The valuation allowance should be sufficient to reduce the deferred tax asset to the
amount that is more likely than not to be realized. As a result, the Company recorded a valuation allowance with respect to all the Company&#146;s deferred tax assets. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The Company has a federal net operating loss (&#147;NOL&#148;) of approximately $93.7 million as of September&nbsp;30, 2011 (expiring beginning in 2020). Under Section&nbsp;382 and 383 of the Internal
Revenue Code, if an ownership occurred change occurs with respect to a &#147;loss&nbsp;corporation&#148;, as defined, there are annual limitations on the amount of the NOL and other deductions which are available to the Company. The portion of the
NOLs incurred prior to June&nbsp;17, 2003 ($3.4 million) is subject to this limitation. As such, these NOLs are limited to approximately $0.2 million per year. NOLs incurred after June&nbsp;17, 2003 may also subject to restriction. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">At the time that the Company was acquired by Accentia, it had significant NOLs. Due to severe limitations of these NOLs and the fact that a significant
portion of NOLs could never be utilized, the Company made and an election under IRS regulation 1.1502-22P32(b)(4) to waive most of the losses. The Company waived approximately $29 million of NOLs while retaining $3.4 million. </FONT></P>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>18. Employee benefit plan </B></FONT></P>
 <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The Accentia 401(k) and Profit Sharing Plan (the &#147;Employee Benefit Plan&#148;) was established effective July&nbsp;1, 2004 as a
defined contribution plan. The Employee Benefit Plan is subject to the provisions of the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 (ERISA). The Employee Benefit Plan includes eligible employees of Accentia and its affiliates including the
Company. Employees of the Company who have completed one month of service are eligible to participate in the Employee Benefit Plan. Participants are permitted to make annual pre-tax salary reduction contributions of up to 50% of their compensation
subject to certain limitations. Employer and participant contributions are invested at the direction of the participant in various money market funds or pooled/mutual funds. Employer matching and profit sharing contributions are based upon
discretionary amounts and percentages authorized by the Board. For the fiscal years ended September&nbsp;30, 2011 and 2010, the Company made no employer contributions to the Employee Benefit Plan. </FONT></P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">F-33
</FONT></P>


<p Style='page-break-before:always'>
<HR  SIZE="3" style="COLOR:#999999" WIDTH="100%" ALIGN="CENTER">
<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>


 <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B></B>BIOVEST INTERNATIONAL, INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES<B> </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B></B>NOTES TO CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS<B> </B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B></B>FOR THE YEARS ENDED SEPTEMBER 30, 2011 AND 2010<B> </B></FONT></P> <p STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></P>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>19. Commitments and contingencies </B></FONT></P>
 <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>Bankruptcy proceedings:</I> </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">On November&nbsp;10, 2008, the Company filed a voluntary petition for reorganization under Chapter 11 of the United States Bankruptcy Code (&#147;Chapter
11&#148;) in the U.S. Bankruptcy Court for the Middle District of Florida, Tampa Division (the &#147;Bankruptcy Court&#148;). On August&nbsp;16, 2010, the Company filed its First Amended Joint Plan of Reorganization, and on October&nbsp;25, 2010,
the Company filed the First Modification to the First Amended Joint Plan of Reorganization (collectively and as amended and supplemented, the &#147;Plan&#148;). On November&nbsp;2, 2010, the Bankruptcy Court entered an Order Confirming Debtors&#146;
First Amended Joint Plan of Reorganization Under Chapter 11 of the Bankruptcy Code (the &#147;Confirmation Order&#148;). The Company emerged from Chapter 11 protection, and the Plan became effective, on November&nbsp;17, 2010 (the &#147;Effective
Date&#148;). Notwithstanding the effectiveness of the Plan, the Bankruptcy Court retains jurisdiction to adjudicate any remaining issues regarding, <I>inter alia,</I> the validity, amount, and method of payment of claims filed in connection with the
Company&#146;s Chapter 11 proceeding. Accordingly, the Company anticipates that there may be ongoing proceedings before the Bankruptcy Court to resolve any filed objections or disputes as to claims filed in the Chapter 11 proceeding. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>Other proceedings:</I> </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">On August&nbsp;4,
2008, the Company was served with a summons and complaint filed in California Superior Court on behalf of Clinstar&nbsp;LLC for breach of contract for non-payment of certain fees for clinical trial studies and pass-through expenses in the amount of
$385,000. The Company intends to seek to dismiss this litigation and plan to defend these claims vigorously. Upon the filing of the Company&#146;s Chapter 11 petition on November&nbsp;10, 2008, this litigation was automatically stayed pursuant to
provisions of federal bankruptcy law. The Company anticipates that the claims involved in this litigation will be contested and resolved by the Bankruptcy Court as part of an objection to claim which the Company expects to file shortly. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Except for the foregoing, the Company is not party to any material legal proceedings, and management is not aware of any threatened legal proceedings
that could cause a material adverse impact on the Company&#146;s business, assets, or results of operations. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>Facility leases:
</I></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">On December&nbsp;2, 2010, the Company entered into a lease agreement (the &#147;Lease&#148;) for its approximate 35,000 square feet
facility in Minneapolis (Coon Rapids), Minnesota, which is used for offices, a laboratory, manufacturing, and warehousing areas to support the production of perfusion cell culture equipment and contract cell culture services. The Lease has a ten
year term and provides for certain improvements to the facility, which have been financed and performed principally by the Landlord, as well as through government grant loans from city and state agencies in Minnesota. These improvements, which were
completed as of September&nbsp;30, 2011, include the construction of a GMP vaccine manufacturing space. Rent expense for the years ended September 30, 2011 and 2010, under the Lease was approximately $0.3 million for each year. Total rent payments
for years 1-5 under the Lease will be $0.43 million per year. Total rent payments for years 6-10 under the Lease will be $0.5 million per year. The Company also has the right to extend the term of the Lease for two additional five year periods at
the greater of base rent in effect at the end of the ten year initial lease term, or market rates in effect at the end of the ten year initial lease term. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The Company also shares office space with the Company&#146;s majority shareholder, Accentia, and utilizes the space as the Company&#146;s principal executive and administrative offices. Accentia leases
approximately 7,400 square feet of office space in Tampa, Florida. The lease expires on September&nbsp;30, 2014 and is cancelable by either party with 120 days prior notice. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px;padding-bottom:0px;"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The Company anticipates that its facilities will meet its needs during fiscal 2012. The Company anticipates that as its development
of BiovaxID<FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUP STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; bottom:.8ex">&reg;</SUP></FONT> advances and as the Company prepares for the future commercialization of its products, its
facilities requirements will increase. </FONT></P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">F-34
</FONT></P>


<p Style='page-break-before:always'>
<HR  SIZE="3" style="COLOR:#999999" WIDTH="100%" ALIGN="CENTER">
<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>


 <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B></B>BIOVEST INTERNATIONAL, INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES<B> </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B></B>NOTES TO CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS<B> </B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B></B>FOR THE YEARS ENDED SEPTEMBER 30, 2011 AND 2010<B> </B></FONT></P> <p STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></P>
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>19. Commitments and contingencies (continued) </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>Cooperative Research and Development Agreement: </I></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">In September 2001, the Company entered into a definitive cooperative research and development agreement (&#147;CRADA&#148;) with the NCI for the development and ultimate commercialization of
patient-specific vaccines for the treatment of non-Hodgkin&#146;s low-grade follicular lymphoma. The terms of the CRADA, as amended, included, among other things, a requirement to pay $0.5 million quarterly to NCI for expenses incurred in connection
with the ongoing Phase 3 clinical trials. Since the transfer to the Company of the IND application for development of this vaccine, which occurred in April 2004, these payments to NCI have been reduced to a small fraction of this original obligation
(approximately $0.2 million per year). On September&nbsp;25, 2006, the Company provided written notice to the NCI in accordance with the terms of the CRADA to terminate the CRADA at the end of the sixty (60)&nbsp;day notice period. Under the terms
of the CRADA, the Company is obligated to continue to provide vaccine to the NCI at no charge for purposes of the NCI&#146;s studies that are within the scope of the CRADA if the Company were to abandon work on the vaccine. As the Company is
actively developing the vaccine for commercialization and intends to do so to its completion, no estimated costs have been accrued as of September&nbsp;30, 2011. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>Food and Drug Administration: </I></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The FDA has extensive regulatory authority over
biopharmaceutical products (drugs and biological products), manufacturing protocols and procedures and the facilities in which mammalian proteins will be manufactured. Any new bioproduct intended for use in humans (including, to a somewhat lesser
degree, in vivo biodiagnostic products), is subject to rigorous testing requirements imposed by the FDA with respect to product efficacy and safety, possible toxicity and side effects. FDA approval for the use of new bioproducts (which can never be
assured) requires several rounds of extensive preclinical testing and clinical investigations conducted by the sponsoring pharmaceutical company prior to sale and use of the product. At each stage, the approvals granted by the FDA include the
manufacturing process utilized to produce the product. Accordingly, the Company&#146;s cell culture systems used for the production of therapeutic or biotherapeutic products are subject to significant regulation by the FDA under the Federal Food,
Drug and Cosmetic Act, as amended (the &#147;FD&amp;C Act&#148;). </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>Royalty agreements: </I></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px;padding-bottom:0px;"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">On the Effective Date and pursuant to the Plan, the Company, Accentia, and Laurus/Valens entered into agreements whereby Accentia
terminated and cancelled all of its royalty interest and Laurus/Valens reduced its royalty interest in BiovaxID<FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUP STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; bottom:.8ex">&reg;</SUP></FONT>
and the Company&#146;s other biologic products. As a result of the foregoing agreements, the aggregate royalty obligation on BiovaxID and the Company&#146;s other biologic products was reduced from 35.25% to 6.30%. Additionally, Laurus/Valens&#146;s
royalty interest on the AutovaxID<FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUP STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; bottom:.8ex">&reg;</SUP></FONT> instrument was reduced from 3.0% to no obligation, including the elimination
of the $7.5 million minimum royalty obligation. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>Product liability: </I></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The contract production services for therapeutic products offered exposes an inherent risk of liability as the proteins or other substances manufactured, at the request and to the specifications of
customers, could foreseeably cause adverse effects. The Company obtains agreements from contract production customers indemnifying and defending the Company from any potential liability arising from such risk. There can be no assurance, however,
that the Company will be successful in obtaining such agreements in the future or that such indemnification agreements will adequately protect the Company against potential claims relating to such contract production services. The Company may also
be exposed to potential product liability claims by users of its products. A successful partial or completely uninsured claim against the Company could have a material adverse effect on the Company&#146;s operations. </FONT></P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">F-35
</FONT></P>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B></B>BIOVEST INTERNATIONAL, INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES<B> </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B></B>NOTES TO CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS<B> </B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B></B>FOR THE YEARS ENDED SEPTEMBER 30, 2011 AND 2010<B> </B></FONT></P> <p STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></P>
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>19. Commitments and contingencies (continued) </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>Stanford University agreement: </I></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px;padding-bottom:0px;"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">In September 2004, the Company entered into an agreement (&#147;Stanford Agreement&#148;) with Stanford University (&#147;Stanford&#148;) allowing worldwide rights to use two proprietary hybridoma cell
lines that are used in the production of BiovaxID<FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUP STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; bottom:.8ex">&reg;</SUP></FONT>. Under the Stanford Agreement, the Company is obligated to
pay an annual maintenance fee of $10,000. The Stanford Agreement also provides that the Company pay Stanford $100,000 within one (1)&nbsp;year following FDA approval of BiovaxID, and following approval, the Company is required to pay Stanford a
running royalty of the higher of $50 per patient or 0.05% of revenues received by the Company for each BiovaxID patient treated using this cell line. This running royalty will be creditable against the yearly maintenance fee. The Company&#146;s
agreement with Stanford obligates the Company to diligently develop, manufacture, market, and sell BiovaxID and to provide progress reports to Stanford regarding these activities. The Company can terminate this agreement at any time upon thirty
(30)&nbsp;days&#146; prior written notice and Stanford can terminate the agreement upon a breach of the agreement by the Company that remains uncured for thirty (30)&nbsp;days after written notice of the breach from Stanford. </FONT></P>
 <P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>20. Subsequent Events </B></FONT></P>
 <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>Appointment and election of certain officers and directors: </I></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Effective as of December 20, 2011, Samuel S. Duffey, Esq., was appointed to serve in the position of Chief Executive Officer, in addition to continuing his responsibilities as the Company&#146;s President
and General Counsel. Mr. Duffey was also designated to serve as the Company&#146;s Principal Executive Officer in connection with dealings with the Company&#146;s independent audit firm and filings with the SEC. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Effective as of December 20, 2011, Francis E. O&#146;Donnell, Jr., M.D. was appointed by the Board of Directors to serve in the position of Executive
Chairman of the Board. </FONT></P>
